title: The amenity basket
text: Read six category ids free catalog, turn city name centre point boundary.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here amenity_types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(amenity_types$dest_type_id, amenity_types$label)  basket <- c(   supermarket = ids[["grocery_stores"]],   library = ids[["libraries"]],   park = ids[["parks"]],   transit = ids[["frequent_transit"]],   restaurant = ids[["restaurants"]],   cafe = ids[["cafes"]] )  city <- close$places(q = "Richmond")[1, ] city_boundary <- close$place_boundary(geoid = city$geoid)

title: The amenity basket
text: $blocks_query() reads every page: walk time every block six categories, plus block’s population, one sf table. keep tutorial cheap take central blocks within radius; $place_blocks(geoid = city$geoid) pulls every block city way (higher token cost).
code: blocks <- close$blocks_query(   center = list(lon = city$lon, lat = city$lat), radius_m = 2500,   mode = "walk", type = unname(basket), include_population = TRUE )  # One row per block, for population and for mapping. one_per_block <- blocks[!duplicated(blocks$geoid), ] total_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population)

what: Coverage, one amenity at a time
title: The amenity basket
text: amenity, block counts covered within 15-minute walk. Add population covered blocks. Parks restaurants tend everywhere; supermarkets libraries usually hardest reach. Map one amenity — every block shown, covered ones highlighted, city boundary behind.
code: for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %3.0f%%\n", name, 100 * pop / total_pop)) } #> supermarket  13% #> library      38% #> park         89% #> transit       5% #> restaurant   71% #> cafe         64% near_library <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket["library"] &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 15]) one_per_block$has_library <- one_per_block$geoid %in% near_library closecity::close_map(x = one_per_block, highlight = "has_library", color = "#058040",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: The amenity basket
text: Count, block, many six amenities within 15-minute walk. score, 0 6, map planners reach ; blue marks best-served blocks. reuses data already pulled, costs nothing .
code: covered <- blocks[blocks$travel_time <= 15, ] score <- tapply(covered$dest_type_id, covered$geoid, function(x) length(unique(x))) one_per_block$score <- as.integer(score[one_per_block$geoid]) one_per_block$score[is.na(one_per_block$score)] <- 0L closecity::close_map(x = one_per_block, fill = "score", boundary = city_boundary)

title: The amenity basket
text: block fully covered six amenities within 15 minutes. Fold per-amenity covered sets together.
code: covered_sets <- lapply(names(basket), function(name) {   unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                         blocks$travel_time <= 15]) }) covered_all <- Reduce(intersect, covered_sets)  basket_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all]) cat(sprintf("All six amenities: %.0f%% of residents\n", 100 * basket_pop / total_pop)) #> All six amenities: 4% of residents  one_per_block$full_basket <- one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all closecity::close_map(x = one_per_block, highlight = "full_basket", color = "#f36e21",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: The amenity basket
text: Look residents yet fully covered, count many missing amenity. amenity people lack one add first.
code: uncovered <- setdiff(one_per_block$geoid, covered_all)  for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   lacking <- setdiff(uncovered, covered)   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% lacking])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %6.0f residents would gain access\n", name, pop)) } #> supermarket  26219 residents would gain access #> library      18720 residents would gain access #> park          3373 residents would gain access #> transit      28424 residents would gain access #> restaurant    8732 residents would gain access #> cafe         10798 residents would gain access

what: Who is one or two amenities away
title: The amenity basket
text: residents worth targeting first ones almost — block five six much easier win one none. Build block--amenity coverage grid, count many amenities block missing, blocks short just one two, break amenities gap.
code:
covered_by <- lapply(names(basket), function(name) {   unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                         blocks$travel_time <= 15]) }) names(covered_by) <- names(basket)  # One logical row per block: is each amenity within 15 minutes? have <- vapply(covered_by, function(s) one_per_block$geoid %in% s,                logical(nrow(one_per_block))) n_missing <- rowSums(!have) gap <- apply(!have, 1, function(row) paste(names(basket)[row], collapse = " + "))  # Residents who are short by exactly one or two amenities. almost <- n_missing %in% c(1, 2) almost_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[almost]) cat(sprintf("%.0f%% of residents are one or two amenities short of the full basket.

",             100 * almost_pop / total_pop)) #> 31% of residents are one or two amenities short of the full basket.  pop_by_gap <- tapply(one_per_block$population[almost], gap[almost], sum) pop_by_gap <- sort(pop_by_gap, decreasing = TRUE) for (g in names(pop_by_gap)) {   cat(sprintf("  missing %-22s %3.0f%%
", g, 100 * pop_by_gap[g] / almost_pop)) } #>   missing supermarket + transit   78% #>   missing transit                 10% #>   missing library + transit        8% #>   missing supermarket              3% #>   missing library                  1%

title: The amenity basket
text: counts say amenity add; next question . Take candidate site land north James River ask many residents newly gain supermarket within 15-minute walk one opened . direction = "" isochrone gives exactly blocks reach site foot 15 minutes. Map whole city highlight blocks newly gain access — population one site reaches.
code: site_lon <- -77.437 site_lat <- 37.548 reachable <- close$isochrone(lon = site_lon, lat = site_lat, mode = "walk",                              direction = "to", minutes = 15, format = "blocks")  near_supermarket <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket["supermarket"] &                                           blocks$travel_time <= 15]) newly_served <- setdiff(reachable$geoid, near_supermarket) gain_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% newly_served]) cat(sprintf("A supermarket here would newly serve %.0f residents\n", gain_pop)) #> A supermarket here would newly serve 0 residents one_per_block$newly_served <- one_per_block$geoid %in% newly_served closecity::close_map(x = one_per_block, highlight = "newly_served", color = "#e8590c",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: Get started with closecity
text: terms come throughout: Census block. smallest area Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id, GEOID. Block GEOIDs come census — look tigris tidycensus packages, Census Bureau geocoder/API, read straight Close’s block routes ($blocks_query(), $place_blocks()). Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. Every type numeric id. Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone catchment: area can reach starting point within time limit, selected travel mode.

title: Get started with closecity
text: Times nearby places capped 30 minutes mode, recorded whole minutes. missing time means place reachable within cap, zero. Isochrones exception: available budget hour.

title: Get started with closecity
text: make every request client object. catalog lookup routes free, close_client() key also works .
code: library(closecity) close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here close$modes() #>   mode_id    mode    description #> 1       1    walk        Walking #> 2       2    bike         Biking #> 3       3 transit Public transit

what: Look things up instead of guessing
title: Get started with closecity
text: Two free calls save memorising codes. come back data frames, filter index usual way: read numeric id category catalog, turn city name GEOID centre point. catalog’s name column readable label (“Grocery stores”); underscored label internal key match . place lookup carries state, can tell Providence, RI one Utah. point rather block, $point_summary(lat = , lon = ) reads travel times lat/lon starting point instead GEOID.
code: amenity_types <- close$destination_types() supermarket_type <- amenity_types[amenity_types$label == "grocery_stores", ]$dest_type_id  providence_ri <- close$places(q = "Providence")[1, ] providence_ri[, c("name", "state", "geoid")] #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 3 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -71.41872 ymin: 41.82301 xmax: -71.41872 ymax: 41.82301 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>         name state   geoid                   geometry #> 1 Providence    RI 4459000 POINT (-71.41872 41.82301)

what: Make a call and map it
title: Get started with closecity
text: Routes geometry return sf object. close_map() draws interactive basemap one line — bright, hoverable points , city boundary behind view zoomed fit.
code: supermarkets <- close$place_pois(geoid = providence_ri$geoid, type = supermarket_type) city_boundary <- close$place_boundary(geoid = providence_ri$geoid) closecity::close_map(x = supermarkets, color = "#e8590c",                      boundary = city_boundary, label = "name")

title: Get started with closecity
text: Every route returns tabular data default: sf object inherently spatial data, data frame otherwise. output setting changes shape — "tabular" never downloads boundaries, "raw" gives underlying reply metering cursor fields. Set client, pass output = one call. block summary, readable category names merged sorted time: …call raw reply, whose results can inspect :
code: walk_times <- close$block_summary(geoid = "440070008001068", mode = "walk") walk_times <- merge(walk_times, amenity_types[, c("dest_type_id", "name")],                     by = "dest_type_id") walk_times[order(walk_times$travel_time), c("name", "travel_time")] #>                                name travel_time #> 18                All transit stops           2 #> 27       Non-frequent transit stops           2 #> 28 Non-frequent other transit stops           2 #> 29              Other transit stops           2 #> 5                       Restaurants           3 #> 6                              Bars           3 #> 9            Cafes and coffee shops           3 #> 17                        Libraries           3 #> 19                            Parks           3 #> 23               Parks (<0.5 acres)           3 #> 32                 Public libraries           3 #> 20              Parks (0.5–1 acres)           4 #> 30               Parks (>0.5 acres)           4 #> 8                    Grocery stores           6 #> 21               Parks (1–10 acres)           7 #> 31                  Parks (>1 acre)           7 #> 15                       Bookstores           8 #> 7                Convenience stores           9 #> 12                       Pharmacies           9 #> 16                       Bike shops           9 #> 33     University/private libraries           9 #> 1                    Public schools          10 #> 4                      High schools          10 #> 24                      Playgrounds          10 #> 13                       Preschools          13 #> 22                Parks (>10 acres)          13 #> 25                         Bakeries          14 #> 10                         Dentists          15 #> 14                Community centers          17 #> 11        Gyms and exercise studios          18 #> 3                    Middle schools          22 #> 26                  Hardware stores          24 #> 2                Elementary schools          26 raw <- close$block_summary(geoid = "440070008001068", mode = "walk", output = "raw") str(raw$results, max.level = 2, list.len = 3) #> List of 33 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 1 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 10 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 5 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 26 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 6 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 22 #>   [list output truncated]

title: Get started with closecity
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one.
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary(geoid = "000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) ) #> block-not-found (404)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Read cafe category id free catalog find city.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here amenity_types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(amenity_types$dest_type_id, amenity_types$label) cafe <- ids[["cafes"]]  city <- close$places(q = "Providence")[1, ] city_boundary <- close$place_boundary(geoid = city$geoid)

what: Find the shops and our walkshed
title: Competitor walksheds
text: $place_pois() returns every cafe within city’s boundary — radius guess. Pick one subject, pull walkshed: every block can walk 10 minutes. Draw walkshed, cafes top shop orange.
code: cafes <- close$place_pois(geoid = city$geoid, type = cafe) ours <- cafes[1, ] ours$name #> [1] "Little Sister" cafes$is_ours <- cafes$dest_id == ours$dest_id  our_shed <- close$poi_catchment(dest_id = ours$dest_id, mode = "walk",                                 max_minutes = 10) walkshed <- sf::st_union(our_shed) closecity::close_map(x = cafes, color = ifelse(cafes$is_ours, "#f36e21", "#202a5b"),                      label = "name", background = walkshed,                      background_color = "#74b9ff", boundary = city_boundary)

what: Who else those blocks can reach
title: Competitor walksheds
text: competitor cafe residents walkshed can also walk . take blocks walkshed, nearest cafes, pull walkshed: two share blocks, residents can reach . our_shed$geoid just vector block ids, overlap plain set intersection. (check nearest cafes keep token cost ; recipe scales .)
code: our_geoids <- our_shed$geoid others <- cafes[!cafes$is_ours, ] dist_to_ours <- as.numeric(sf::st_distance(others, ours)) nearest <- others[order(dist_to_ours)[seq_len(min(15, nrow(others)))], ]  shared <- vapply(nearest$dest_id, function(id) {   their_shed <- close$poi_catchment(dest_id = id, mode = "walk", max_minutes = 10)   length(intersect(our_geoids, their_shed$geoid)) }, integer(1))  competing <- nearest[shared > 0, ] for (i in order(shared, decreasing = TRUE)) {   if (shared[i] == 0) next   cat(sprintf("%-28s %3d shared blocks (%.0f%% of ours)\n",               nearest$name[i], shared[i],               100 * shared[i] / length(our_geoids))) } #> Cafe Zoey                     90 shared blocks (85% of ours) #> Seven Stars Bakery            79 shared blocks (75% of ours) #> Three Sisters                 11 shared blocks (10% of ours)  cafes$is_competitor <- cafes$dest_id %in% competing$dest_id

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Draw every cafe walkshed: shop orange, competitors blocks can also reach red, rest navy. red cafes ones fighting walk-traffic. recipe scales : raise cafe count check, compare whole cities pulling one’s cafes $place_pois().
code: cafe_color <- ifelse(cafes$is_ours, "#f36e21",                      ifelse(cafes$is_competitor, "#e03131", "#202a5b")) closecity::close_map(x = cafes, color = cafe_color, label = "name",                      background = walkshed, background_color = "#74b9ff",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: Your first walkability map
text: Build client, read need free catalog.
code: library(closecity) close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here amenity_types <- close$destination_types() supermarket_type <- amenity_types[amenity_types$label == "grocery_stores", ]$dest_type_id  providence_ri <- close$places(q = "Providence")[1, ] start_lon <- providence_ri$lon start_lat <- providence_ri$lat

what: Read travel times from a starting point
title: Your first walkability map
text: Pick starting point — centre Providence — ask long takes walk kind amenity. $point_summary() takes lat/lon instead block GEOID. Join catalog’s readable name sort time, nearest things top.
code: walk_times <- close$point_summary(lat = start_lat, lon = start_lon, mode = "walk") walk_times <- merge(walk_times, amenity_types[, c("dest_type_id", "name")],                     by = "dest_type_id") walk_times[order(walk_times$travel_time), c("name", "travel_time")] #>                                name travel_time #> 18                All transit stops           0 #> 27       Non-frequent transit stops           0 #> 28 Non-frequent other transit stops           0 #> 29              Other transit stops           0 #> 5                       Restaurants           1 #> 9            Cafes and coffee shops           1 #> 19                            Parks           2 #> 23               Parks (<0.5 acres)           2 #> 6                              Bars           3 #> 17                        Libraries           3 #> 32                 Public libraries           3 #> 8                    Grocery stores           6 #> 20              Parks (0.5–1 acres)           6 #> 21               Parks (1–10 acres)           6 #> 30               Parks (>0.5 acres)           6 #> 31                  Parks (>1 acre)           6 #> 12                       Pharmacies           7 #> 15                       Bookstores           8 #> 16                       Bike shops           8 #> 7                Convenience stores           9 #> 24                      Playgrounds           9 #> 33     University/private libraries           9 #> 1                    Public schools          11 #> 4                      High schools          11 #> 22                Parks (>10 acres)          12 #> 13                       Preschools          13 #> 25                         Bakeries          13 #> 14                Community centers          14 #> 10                         Dentists          15 #> 11        Gyms and exercise studios          18 #> 3                    Middle schools          21 #> 2                Elementary schools          25 #> 26                  Hardware stores          25

what: Map the supermarkets within a 30-minute walk
title: Your first walkability map
text: 30-minute walk travel-time question, distance one, let routing answer directly: $point_pois() returns every POI reachable starting point within max_minutes, carrying walk time — isochrone overlay. close_map() draws result interactive basemap one line, city boundary behind context.
code: nearby_supermarkets <- close$point_pois(lat = start_lat, lon = start_lon,                                         mode = "walk", type = supermarket_type,                                         max_minutes = 30)  city_boundary <- close$place_boundary(geoid = providence_ri$geoid) closecity::close_map(x = nearby_supermarkets, color = "#e8590c",                      boundary = city_boundary, label = "name")

what: Draw how far you can walk
title: Your first walkability map
text: isochrone headline map: area can reach foot 10, 20, 30 minutes. Shade contour minutes; blue marks nearest, -reachable ring.
code: rings <- close$isochrone(lon = start_lon, lat = start_lat, mode = "walk",                          direction = "from", contours = c(10, 20, 30),                          format = "geojson") closecity::close_map(x = rings, fill = "contour", reverse = TRUE)

title: Your first walkability map
text: starting point 30-minute budget, foot bus — clearest way see transit buys .
code: walk <- close$isochrone(lon = start_lon, lat = start_lat, mode = "walk",                         direction = "from", minutes = 30, format = "geojson") transit <- close$isochrone(lon = start_lon, lat = start_lat, mode = "transit",                            direction = "from", minutes = 30, format = "geojson")  closecity::close_map(x = walk, color = "#058040") closecity::close_map(x = transit, color = "#202a5b")

title: Your first walkability map
text: Looking home: find blocks near several amenities , narrow commute. amenity basket: population-weighted walkability coverage across whole city. Competitor walksheds: else draws neighbourhood. ```

title: Looking for a home
text: Build client, read pieces need free catalog instead memorising codes.
code: library(closecity) library(sf) close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here # The catalog lists every category with its numeric id. Pull the ids you need. amenity_types <- close$destination_types() ids <- setNames(amenity_types$dest_type_id, amenity_types$label)  supermarket_dest_id <- ids[["grocery_stores"]] restaurant_dest_id <- ids[["restaurants"]] freq_transit_stop_dest_id <- ids[["frequent_transit"]]  # Turn the city name into a GEOID and pull its boundary for context. city <- close$places(q = "Somerville")[1, ] city_boundary <- close$place_boundary(geoid = city$geoid)

title: Looking for a home
text: Look raw ingredients first: every supermarket, restaurant, frequent-transit stop within Somerville, $place_pois() — city boundary, guessed radius, edge. Give category colour map together.
code: supermarkets <- close$place_pois(geoid = city$geoid, type = supermarket_dest_id) restaurants <- close$place_pois(geoid = city$geoid, type = restaurant_dest_id) stops <- close$place_pois(geoid = city$geoid, type = freq_transit_stop_dest_id)  supermarkets$kind <- "Supermarket" restaurants$kind <- "Restaurant" stops$kind <- "Transit stop" around <- rbind(supermarkets[, "kind"], restaurants[, "kind"], stops[, "kind"])  palette <- c(Supermarket = "#058040", Restaurant = "#c6cbe0",              `Transit stop` = "#f36e21") closecity::close_map(x = around, color = palette[around$kind], label = "kind",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: Looking for a home
text: Somerville census place, one call place GEOID pulls per-block walk times every block city — $place_blocks() reads every page returns one sf row per (block, category); block boundaries come tigris, downloaded cached. (search arbitrary area instead, use $blocks_query() centre radius polygon — radius tutorials keep token cost low; place GEOID pulls whole city.) Reshape one row per block, walk-time column amenity, block carries three times (hover map shows ). flag blocks pass every rule. Show every block city, highlight ones qualify, hover block read walk time amenity.
code: blocks <- close$place_blocks(geoid = city$geoid, mode = "walk",                              type = c(supermarket_dest_id, restaurant_dest_id,                                       freq_transit_stop_dest_id)) city_blocks <- blocks[!duplicated(blocks$geoid), "geoid"] time_to <- function(type_id) {   sub <- blocks[blocks$dest_type_id == type_id, ]   setNames(sub$travel_time, sub$geoid)[city_blocks$geoid] } city_blocks$supermarket_min <- time_to(supermarket_dest_id) city_blocks$restaurant_min <- time_to(restaurant_dest_id) city_blocks$transit_min <- time_to(freq_transit_stop_dest_id)  city_blocks$qualifies <- (city_blocks$supermarket_min <= 10 &                           city_blocks$restaurant_min <= 5 &                           city_blocks$transit_min <= 20) city_blocks$qualifies[is.na(city_blocks$qualifies)] <- FALSE closecity::close_map(x = city_blocks, highlight = "qualifies", color = "#f36e21",                      boundary = city_boundary)

title: Looking for a home
text: Suppose two work different places. transit isochrone workplace shows far commute reaches; drawn together, half-transparent, can see . Keep qualifying blocks also sit inside commutes. final map shows winning blocks, shortlist — inside commutes — highlighted, two commute walksheds.
code: work_a <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0865, lat = 42.3625, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20, format = "geojson") work_b <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0589, lat = 42.3555, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20, format = "geojson")  closecity::close_map(x = work_a, color = "#058040", opacity = 0.5,                      background = work_b, background_color = "#f36e21",                      background_opacity = 0.5) both_commutes <- sf::st_intersection(sf::st_union(work_a), sf::st_union(work_b)) winners <- city_blocks[city_blocks$qualifies, ] winners$shortlist <- sf::st_intersects(winners, both_commutes, sparse = FALSE)[, 1]  closecity::close_map(x = winners, highlight = "shortlist", color = "#1f78b4",                      boundary = city_boundary,                      background = list(work_a, work_b),                      background_color = c("#058040", "#f36e21"),                      background_opacity = 0.5)

title: Efficient token use
text: Metadata queries free keyless: $modes(), $destination_types(), $vintage(), $places(), $isochrone_meta(), $last_updated(), $health(). lookups spend token. Revalidation free . Keep reply’s etag pass back if_none_match; unchanged response returns free 304. Read output = "raw" want etag:
code: close$output <- "raw" first <- close$block_summary(geoid = "440070008001068", mode = "walk") again <- close$block_summary(geoid = "440070008001068", mode = "walk",                              if_none_match = first$etag) again$not_modified   # TRUE, and nothing was charged #> [1] TRUE

title: Efficient token use
text: rows query returns multiply modes times types times blocks: Pass mode. Omitting returns three modes, three times rows. Filter type. POI areal routes take destination type, ask server category want rather fetching everything filtering R. Look id $destination_types(). Request leaf types, parents. parent type expands leaves, single leaf id fraction rows parent like parks. Shrink area. Cost grows square radius_m, halving radius roughly quarter tokens. Use max_minutes. drops rows filter anyway.

what: Isochrones are the cheap reach primitive
title: Efficient token use
text: isochrone 10 tokens per contour regardless area, format = "blocks" returns every reachable block minutes. whole walkshed 10 tokens, catchment block query charges per block:
code: shed <- close$isochrone(block = "440070008001068", minutes = 30, mode = "walk",                         format = "blocks")

title: Efficient token use
text: Every metered reply carries token counts. frame output modes attached attributes; output = "raw" reply. type filter asks supermarkets, pay handful rows, every POI radius: want numbers, output = "tabular" skips block-boundary download entirely. block boundaries spatial mode joins cached locally, re-running script costs time, tokens; pass block_geometry frame already skip even .
code: close$output <- "tabular" types <- close$destination_types() supermarket_dest_type <- types[types$label == "grocery_stores", ]$dest_type_id  supermarkets <- close$pois_search(lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1200,                                   type = supermarket_dest_type) attr(supermarkets, "tokens_charged") #> [1] 2 attr(supermarkets, "tokens_remaining") #> [1] 999681522

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: R client Close API. Get travel times every US census block nearby places, foot, bike, public transit. data behind close.city, read Close API. Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("henryspatialanalysis/closecity-r")

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: make requests client object. Feature results come back sf objects, can map right away. close_map() draws result interactive CARTO Positron basemap one line — bright hoverable points, blocks shaded travel time, zoomed data. (image snapshot; session tutorials map live pannable.)  Catalog lookup routes free need key:
code: library(closecity) # The key (ck_live_) comes from https://account.close.city (5,000 free tokens, # no card). Or set the CLOSECITY_KEY environment variable and call # close_client() with no argument. close <- closecity::close_client(api_key = "ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here # Supermarkets within a 1.5 km walk of a point (type 30 is grocery stores): supermarkets <- close$pois_search(lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1500, type = 30) closecity::close_map(x = supermarkets, color = "#e8590c", label = "name") close$modes()                       # walk, bike, transit #>   mode_id    mode    description #> 1       1    walk        Walking #> 2       2    bike         Biking #> 3       3 transit Public transit close$places(q = "Providence")      # a city name to its GEOID and centre #> Simple feature collection with 9 features and 5 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -111.8133 ymin: 32.34238 xmax: -71.35821 ymax: 42.28133 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>                 name   geoid state        lon      lat #> 1         Providence 4459000    RI  -71.41872 41.82301 #> 2         Providence 4962360    UT -111.81331 41.70341 #> 3 Providence Village 4859748    TX  -96.95432 33.23890 #> 4         Providence 2163372    KY  -87.75094 37.39935 #> 5         Providence 0162688    AL  -87.77611 32.34238 #> 6    East Providence 4422960    RI  -71.35821 41.80049 #> 7     New Providence 3451810    NJ  -74.40343 40.69964 #> 8    Lake Providence 2241400    LA  -91.18247 32.81322 #> 9     New Providence 1956415    IA  -93.17162 42.28133 #>                     geometry #> 1 POINT (-71.41872 41.82301) #> 2 POINT (-111.8133 41.70341) #> 3  POINT (-96.95432 33.2389) #> 4 POINT (-87.75094 37.39935) #> 5 POINT (-87.77611 32.34238) #> 6 POINT (-71.35821 41.80049) #> 7 POINT (-74.40343 40.69964) #> 8 POINT (-91.18247 32.81322) #> 9 POINT (-93.17162 42.28133)

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Census block. smallest area Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id called GEOID. Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. type numeric id. Look close$destination_types(). Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone catchment: area can reach starting point within time limit, selected travel mode.

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Set output client, per call: output = "spatial" (default) returns sf object geometry applies data.frame otherwise. Block routes join census-block boundaries tigris package, downloaded cached. output = "tabular" returns data.frame every route never downloads boundaries. Reach want numbers. output = "raw" returns underlying close_reply, parsed body $data token counts alongside.
code: close$output <- "raw" reply <- close$block_summary(geoid = "440070008001068", mode = "walk") str(reply$results, max.level = 2, list.len = 3) #> List of 33 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 1 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 10 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 5 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 26 #>  $ :List of 3 #>   ..$ dest_type_id: int 6 #>   ..$ mode        : chr "walk" #>   ..$ travel_time : num 22 #>   [list output truncated]

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one close_api_tokens_exhausted. client retry automatically. rate-limit service-unavailable error, wait e$retry_after seconds (Retry-header) retry request .
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary(geoid = "000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) ) #> block-not-found (404)

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
text: Turns close_reply plain data.frame, one row per record. client returns "tabular" output mode; close_as_sf() adds geometry top rows. Metering envelope metadata (token counts, block_geoid, assumptions, ...) attached attributes.

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
code: close_as_df(x, key = NULL)

title: Convert a Close reply to a data.frame — close_as_df
text: x (close_reply) reply, list shape. key (character(1), default NULL) Name record array read. Detected body omitted.

text: data.frame. Summary replies gain broadcast geoid column.

what: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: Detects geometry payload. POI replies ($pois_search(), $block_pois(), $point_pois(), $poi(), $places()) become points lat/lon. isochrone reply format = "geojson" becomes polygons. Block replies ($blocks_query(), $place_blocks(), $poi_catchment(), isochrone format = "blocks") carry GEOIDs, block boundaries joined block_geometry, downloaded tigris fetch = TRUE.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: close_as_sf(   x,   block_geometry = NULL,   geoid_col = "GEOID20",   crs = 4326,   fetch = FALSE )

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: x (close_reply) reply, list shape. block_geometry (sf, default NULL) Block boundaries geoid_col column, joined block replies 15-digit GEOID. geoid_col (character(1), default 'GEOID20') Name GEOID column block_geometry (TIGER 2020 blocks). crs (default 4326) Coordinate reference system point polygon geometry. fetch (logical(1), default FALSE) TRUE block_geometry NULL, download needed TIGER blocks tigris (inferring state county GEOIDs).

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: sf data frame. Metering envelope metadata attached attributes.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client(output = 'raw') close_as_sf(close$pois_search(lat = 41.82, lon = -71.41, radius_m = 1500)) } # }

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
code: close_client(   api_key = NULL,   base_url = DEFAULT_BASE_URL,   timeout = 30,   output = "spatial" )

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: api_key (character(1), default NULL) API key, NULL free routes. NULL, CLOSECITY_KEY environment variable used set. base_url (character(1)) API base URL. timeout (numeric(1), default 30) Request timeout, seconds. output (character(1), default 'spatial') results come back: 'spatial' returns sf object geometry applies data frame otherwise; 'tabular' returns data frame every route never downloads block boundaries; 'raw' returns close_reply.

text: CloseClient. Make calls methods.

what: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
title: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
text: Draw sf object client method returns interactive map CARTO Positron basemap. Points (POIs, places) render bright hoverable markers; polygons (census blocks) filled, optionally greying features meet criterion ones matter stand . view auto-zooms fit every layer margin, hover shows attributes.

title: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
code: close_map(   x,   color = "#e8590c",   highlight = NULL,   fill = NULL,   palette = "YlGnBu",   reverse = FALSE,   label = NULL,   size = 9,   opacity = 0.65,   boundary = NULL,   background = NULL,   background_color = "#3b6fb0",   background_opacity = 0.3,   buffer = 0.15,   zoom = NULL )

title: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
text: x sf client method — points polygons. color Marker/fill colour flat features (highlighted ones). highlight Optional. logical vector (length nrow(x)) name logical/0-1 column x. supplied, features meet render grey (#888) rest use color — can show every block study area pick matches, rather dropping others. fill Optional. name numeric column shade features , continuous ColorBrewer scale legend (e.g. travel time, access score). Use highlight, . palette plotly ColorBrewer colorscale name fill (default "YlGnBu"). reverse Reverse fill colorscale (default FALSE, blue end YlGnBu marks high values). Pass TRUE high values mean less access, e.g. travel time, blue still marks -accessible end. label Optional. column shown first (bold) hover; rest attributes follow. size Marker size, point maps. opacity Fill opacity, polygon maps. boundary Optional. polygon sf drawn grey outline underneath data — e.g. city boundary place_boundary(). background Optional. polygon sf, list , drawn semi-transparent fills underneath data — e.g. commute isochrones, walkshed POIs. background_color Fill colour(s) background, recycled across layers. background_opacity Fill opacity background layers. buffer Fraction data extent pad view (default 0.15). zoom Deprecated/ignored; view auto-zooms data.

title: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
text: plotly map object.

title: Interactive map of Close spatial results — close_map
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client() close_map(close$place_pois(geoid = "4459000", type = 30), color = "#e8590c") } # }

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: output 'raw', every method returns close_reply: list parsed body plus metering caching information named fields.

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: data parsed body (list), NULL 304. results results array list routes (else empty list). next_cursor cursor next page, NULL. tokens_charged, tokens_remaining Token counts; NULL free routes. etag response ETag, pass back if_none_match. status HTTP status code. not_modified TRUE 304 (data NULL). request_id Server request id, handy support.

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: Print close_reply

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
code: # S3 method for class 'close_reply' print(x, ...)

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: x close_reply. ... Ignored.

title: Print a close_reply — print.close_reply
text: x, invisibly.

title: closecity 1.4.0
text: $place_boundary() — boundary polygon census place, one-row sf. Free (API key); handy boundary layer close_map(). close_map() gains layered context smarter defaults: auto-zooms data, shows every attribute hover, defaults ColorBrewer YlGnBu scale (blue = accessible), takes boundary outline semi-transparent background layers (e.g. city boundary, commute isochrones, walkshed POIs).

title: closecity 1.3.0
text: $place_pois() — every point interest within census place (city town), place GEOID. place analog $pois_search(); pass type get, e.g., supermarkets city. close_map() — one-line interactive map (CARTO Positron basemap, hoverable points, filled blocks highlight features meeting criterion) sf objects client returns. Built plotly (Suggests dependency), needs GDAL beyond sf already uses. $places() results now carry state column (two-letter USPS abbreviation), -named places distinguishable.

title: closecity 1.2.0
text: close_client() reads CLOSECITY_KEY environment variable api_key given. 401 key set carries actionable hint pointing CLOSECITY_KEY free-signup page. Paginated methods ($pois_search(), $block_pois(), $point_pois(), $poi_catchment(), $blocks_query(), $place_blocks()) read every page default, matching Python client. Pass paginate = FALSE first page , explicit cursor single page. $records() kept equivalent explicit form. client retry: rate-limit service-unavailable error, wait e$retry_after seconds retry request .

title: closecity 1.1.0
text: Tabular results default, across every route. New output argument replaces spatial: close_client(output = "spatial") (default) returns sf object geometry applies plain data frame catalog summary routes; output = "tabular" returns data frame every route never downloads block boundaries (cheap path want numbers); output = "raw" returns close_reply. Set client per call. Catalog routes ($modes(), $destination_types(), $vintage(), $places()) block point summaries now return data frames. $block_summary() / $point_summary() broadcast origin GEOID geoid column, $isochrone(format = "blocks") now converts . Metering envelope metadata (token counts, block_geoid, assumptions, …) attached attributes returned frame. New close_as_df() .data.frame() method close_reply, beside existing close_as_sf(). Requires R >= 4.1 (native pipe used internally).

title: closecity 1.0.0
text: First public release closecity R client Close API (api.close.city). R6 client, close_client(), one method per public route: catalog, place lookup, block point summaries POIs, POI search, detail, catchment, areal block queries, isochrones. Feature methods return sf objects default. Set spatial = FALSE work raw reply. Block boundaries joined tigris. First-class metering (tokens_charged, tokens_remaining), ETag/304 conditional requests, keyset pagination via $records(), typed RFC 9457 errors classed conditions. $places() looks city town name returns GEOID centre.
